questions.question: What actress from the TV show Roseanne went on to play the mother of Sheldon Cooper in the Big Bang Theory?
What country artist played Daisy Duke in The Dukes of Hazard movie remake, and famously asked if tuna was "chicken or fish" on her reality show?
Finish these lyrics by TLC: "A scrub is a guy that and is also known as a buster."
In 1997, IBM's Deep Blue defeated this world chess champion.
What did it mean if someone called you a GOAT?
In the animated version of Aladdin, what did Aladdin steal that prompted the guards to begin chasing him?
Just like its name, these vending machines offered movie rentals.
This "Songbird Supreme" has a five-octave vocal range and had a string of number one hits for 11 straight years from 1989 to 2000.
Name the two Presidential candidates in the controversial 2000 election.
What snack brand's slogan was "Once you pop, you can't stop" referencing the sound their packaging made when opened?
Name Pa Cartwright's three sons on the TV show Bonanza.
Who invented the first frozen TV dinners in 1954?
Jim Morrison, Ray Manzarek, Robby Krieger and John Densmore made up what band?
Name two of the three rock and roll musicians killed in a 1959 plane crash in Clear Lake, Iowa.
questions.answer: Buddy Holly, Ritchie Valens, "The Big Bopper" J.P. Richardson

questions.question: In 1971, what character said, "You've got to ask yourself one question: 'Do I feel lucky?' Well, do ya, punk?"
Which Tanner daughter famously exclaimed, "How rude" in every episode?
What service, launched in 1997, allowed subscribers to have DVDs mailed directly to their home?
What was Marshall Bruce Mathers III's stage name?
What five-year-old boy became a catalyst on immigration policy with a standoff between the US and Cuba in 2000.
What did WMD stand for?
Whose time travel adventures had them meeting historical figures like "So-crates"?
What toilet paper were grocery shoppers discouraged from squeezing?
What bearded group was known for "Sharp Dressed Man" and "Legs"?
Who was murdered by Mark David Chapman in 1980?
Used to describe a married couple, what did the acronym DINK mean?
Name the 80's ensemble police drama, led by Captain Frank Furillo, that received eight Emmy Awards in its initial season.
What singer became a spokesperson for the Psychic Friends Network astrology hotline?
Name the movie that made a hit song of an electronic instrumental piece by Harold Faltermeyer.
Organized by Quincy Jones, what was the 1985 superstar charity recording for famine relief efforts in Ethiopia?
In the 70s, what did it mean if someone asked, "What's your 20?"
questions.answer: What's your location or where are you?

questions.question: In what Disney show did an older sister give her baby sister advice through recordings?
What e-cigarette was popular with teenagers because of its small size that looked like a USB?
What 2019 song by Luis Fonsi (ft. Daddy Yankee), reached the milestones of 2 billion and 2.5 billion views the fastest in YouTube's history?
Name the hippo born at the Cincinnati Zoo in 2017 who became world famous through social media feeds chronicling her progress.
What TV show used the phrase, "The Tribe has spoken"?
The Honey Boo Boo show was a major circus and viewers jumped on the crazy train to watch. What was her mom's name on the show?
Name the hero of the book series by Rick Riordan that follows a boy demigod, who is the son of Poseidon and a mortal woman.
Finish the lyrics: "Welcome to the final show, __________."
Name the person convicted of murdering her ex-boyfriend in 2008, leaving incriminating evidence on a digital camera in his apartment?
What was meant by the phrase, "Turned down for what"?
This 1950s children's show had a Peanut Gallery and a clown named Clarabell.
What popular building toy was invented by Frank Lloyd Wright's son to inspire kids to be creative?
What did Simon and Garfunkel ask you to buy if you were going to Scarborough Fair?
Name the man who was the first African-American to receive the Nobel Peace Prize, primarily for his work as a peace negotiator in the Middle East following the creation of Israel.
What tagline was associated with the Smothers Brothers?
What game show invented by Merv Griffin and his wife in 1964 gave contestants the answer and the contestants had to provide the question?
This author's revolutionary book Baby and Child Care was a top seller for decades.
Name three of the five original members of the Jackson 5.
What famous female athlete, considered the greatest of the first half of the 20th century, was an Olympic gold medalist and professional golfer?
What was a "patsy"?
questions.answer: Scapegoat; someone left to face consequences; a gullible person

questions.question: What famous on-screen couple starred in Send Me No Flowers, Lover Come Back, and Pillow Talk?
Name the author of the science fiction anti-war novel Slaughterhouse-Five, published in 1969.
Based on a Dickens' novel, this 1960s musical was a smash hit on stage and screen, with songs "Food, Glorious Food," "Consider Yourself," and "As Long as He Needs Me."
What item did Nikita Khrushchev supposedly pound when agitated at the United Nations in 1960?
In what 1972 movie was the iconic phrase, "I'm gonna make him an offer he can't refuse"?
In what club were Claire, Andrew, John, Brian and Allison?
What airline was known for its use of Gershwin's "Rhapsody in Blue"?
Name the iconic Irish band and their most famous album in 1987, named for a National Park in California.
Where did The Experimental Prototype Community of Tomorrow open in 1982?
If someone said "no duh," what were they implying?
questions.answer: They already knew something, or it was common knowledge
